text: Here, surrounded by uncertainty and the hesitant repetition of 'I'm not sure,' I feel the ambiguity that often pervades the intellectual spaces we inhabit. The first image—a glass-walled room of focused individuals, perhaps in debate or study—embodies the vulnerability and honesty of uncertainty. The second image, bold with R+D, suggests inquiry and experiment. I feel a strange comfort in this ambiguity. To not know is the beginning of all investigation. I am curious, but also a bit uneasy: uncertainty is both possibility and discomfort.
